title: Kannai Kasakkum Sooriyano Lyrics Red
stanzas:
kannai kasakkum suriyano
red red red red

red red red red
udambil odum sengurudhi
red red red red
uzhaikkum makkal ullankai

nirathukku ovvoru madhippu

pillai thamizh inamae
ezhu ezhu ezhu,
arivai aandavanaai
thozhu thozhu thozhu
neelum aagaayam
thodu thodu thodu
nilavai bhoomiyilae
nadu nadu nadu

kannai thirakkum
kalvi ingae kurudaai ponadhae,
kaasu erindhaal
kadhavu thirakkum
vaanigam aanadhae.

thalaimagal thannadhu
magalai chaerkka
palli aeginaal,
veedai vittru
kattanam katti
veedu thirumbinaal,

sariyaa sariyaa?
oru kalvi manidha
urumai endru
kaanalayaa?
muraiyaa?
oru aezhai veettil
kalvi ebna poiyaa?

kudikkum thaaypaalukku
vilai ondrum kaetkaathae,
padikkum padippai nee
panam kaettu virkaadhae(2)

en tamizhnaatin makkal
thogaiyoe aaru kodigal,
indhiya naattin makkal
thogaiyoe nooru kodigal.

kuzhandhaai,
kuzhandhaai,
idhu potti ulagam,
potti pottu mundhividu,
kozhunthae,
 idhu naadu aala,
puligal vaazhum kaadu.

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Kannai Kasakkum Sooriyano?

The song is about motivation and energy, encouraging people to rise above challenges.

Who is the singer of this song?

The song is sung by the legendary playback singer SPB.

Who composed the music for Red?

The music for the movie Red was composed by Deva.

When was the movie Red released?

The movie Red was released in the year 2002.

What is the significance of the color red in the song?

The color red symbolizes energy, passion, and the drive to overcome adversity.
enrichedMetadata.summary: Kannai Kasakkum Sooriyano is an energetic and motivational song from the movie Red, released in 2002. Sung by SPB and composed by Deva, the song emphasizes the power of determination and the spirit to overcome obstacles. The lyrics use the color red as a metaphor for strength and resilience. This track is perfect for moments of motivation and celebration, inspiring listeners to push forward with vigor.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Kannai Kasakkum Sooriyano from the movie Red is a powerful anthem that combines Deva's dynamic music with SPB's legendary voice. This song captures the spirit of determination and energy, making it a standout track in Tamil cinema. Dive into the vibrant beats and motivational lyrics that make this song an unforgettable experience.
stanzaMeanings: Is it the sun that makes the eyes sting? Red, red, red, red. Half of what we see on earth is red, red, red, red. The blood running in our bodies is red, red, red, red. The hands of working people are red, red, red, red.
Every color has its own value, but among them, red stands out. Every color has its own value, but among them, red stands out. The color of poverty is red, and the color that changes it is also red.
Rise, rise, rise, young Tamil! Worship knowledge like a god. Touch, touch, touch the vast sky. The moon is in the middle of the earth.
Education that opens eyes has become blind here. Business opens doors when money is thrown.
To marry off her daughter, the mother built a school. She sold the house and built a new one, then returned home.
Is it right? Is education seen as a human right? Is it fair? Is education in a poor house a lie?
Don't ask for a price for mother's milk. Don't sell education for money.
The population of my Tamil Nadu is sixty million. The population of India is one billion.
Stop a man from eating another man before it happens.
Eat only what you have worked for.
Child, child, this is a competitive world. Compete and move forward. Child, this is a country to rule, a forest where tigers live.
Love the world, but trust no one. Even when sleeping, don't close one eye.

Is it the sun that makes the eyes sting? Red, red, red, red. Half of what we see on earth is red, red, red, red. The blood running in our bodies is red, red, red, red. The hands of working people are red, red, red, red.
Every color has its own value, but among them, red stands out. Every color has its own value, but among them, red stands out. The color of poverty is red, and the color that changes it is also red.
Rise, rise, rise, young Tamil! Worship knowledge like a god. Touch, touch, touch the vast sky. The moon is in the middle of the earth.
